per_question.answer_preview: Extension plugins must provide a ChannelPlugin object. The ClawdbotPluginApi.registerChannel() method registers them, which accepts either a ChannelPlugin directly or a ClawdbotPluginChannelRegistrati

per_question.answer_preview: The CHAT_CHANNEL_ORDER array in src/channels/registry.ts defines the core channels in order: telegram, whatsapp, discord, googlechat, slack, signal, imessage. Channel aliases are normalized via CHAT_C

per_question.expected_preview: src/channels/plugins/ (lines 1-78 in src/channels/plugins/index.ts) contains the plugin infrastructu
per_question.answer_preview: src/channels/plugins/ contains core bundled channel implementations (telegram, discord, slack, etc.). extensions/ contains external plugin channels. Core channels are part of the main distribution; ex

per_question.expected_preview: The src/routing/session-key.ts module (lines 1-212) is responsible for building session keys. It exp
per_question.answer_preview: src/routing/session-key.ts (imported at src/channels/dock.ts:8) exports normalizeAccountId and DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID. Session keys are built from user identity, channel, and account information.

per_question.expected_preview: The src/plugins/runtime/types.ts module (lines 1-362) exports the PluginRuntime type. The four top-l
per_question.answer_preview: src/plugins/runtime/types.ts exports PluginRuntime. It provides top-level namespaces accessible to plugins through the ClawdbotPluginApi.runtime property.

per_question.expected_preview: src/gateway/server-http.ts:59-200 defines createHooksRequestHandler which handles HTTP webhook reque
per_question.answer_preview: server-http.ts handles HTTP protocol setup; hooks.ts provides event/lifecycle hooks. The gateway server uses hooks to integrate custom request processing logic.

per_question.expected_preview: The createApi function is defined in src/plugins/registry.ts:464-495. It returns a ClawdbotPluginApi
per_question.answer_preview: createApi is defined in src/plugins/registry.ts:464-495. It exposes: registerTool, registerHook, registerHttpHandler, registerHttpRoute, registerChannel, registerProvider, registerGatewayMethod, regis

per_question.expected_preview: src/agents/schema/typebox.ts (lines 1-44) provides helper functions for building agent tool schemas
per_question.answer_preview: src/agents/schema/typebox.ts provides TypeBox schema utilities for agent tools. It exports stringEnum and optionalStringEnum helpers for defining schema enums (re-exported via plugin-sdk).

per_question.expected_preview: The plugin system uses a runtime injection pattern. Extensions/discord/index.ts:13 calls setDiscordR
per_question.answer_preview: Extension plugins export a runtime.ts module that provides setup logic, and index.ts acts as the entry point that imports the runtime and registers components via ClawdbotPluginApi.

per_question.expected_preview: src/channels/dock.ts uses a static DOCKS constant (lines 91-372) mapping ChatChannelId to ChannelDoc
per_question.answer_preview: DOCKS is a Record<ChatChannelId, ChannelDock> constant defining core channel metadata and behavior. Fallback: listPluginDockEntries() uses buildDockFromPlugin() to generate docks for plugin channels m

per_question.expected_preview: src/auto-reply/reply/provider-dispatcher.ts (lines 1-45) exports high-level dispatcher functions lik
per_question.answer_preview: provider-dispatcher.ts handles channel-specific reply delivery logic per provider. dispatch.ts coordinates overall reply dispatch routing across all channels.

per_question.expected_preview: According to comments at src/channels/dock.ts:81-86, the dock module must remain 'light' and NOT imp
per_question.answer_preview: dock.ts must not import: (1) channel plugins (heavy), (2) monitors/probes, (3) web login/auth logic. This keeps dock.ts lightweight for shared code to import without pulling in large dependencies.

per_question.expected_preview: The PluginRegistry type is defined in src/plugins/registry.ts:124-138. It maintains 11 collection ar
per_question.answer_preview: PluginRegistry maintains: plugins, tools, hooks, typedHooks, channels, providers, httpHandlers, httpRoutes, cliRegistrars, services, commands, diagnostics arrays storing different registration types.

per_question.expected_preview: src/index.ts (lines 1-95) is the main entry point (shebang at line 1). It loads environment (loadDot
per_question.answer_preview: src/index.ts uses buildProgram() from src/cli/program.js which uses Commander.js (node:process, node:fs imports). It sets up CLI with global error handlers.

per_question.expected_preview: src/plugin-sdk/index.ts (lines 1-372) serves as the public Plugin SDK entrypoint, re-exporting types
per_question.answer_preview: src/plugin-sdk/index.ts serves as public API for plugins, re-exporting: ChannelPlugin, ClawdbotPluginApi, PluginRuntime, channel helpers, config types, and utility functions.

per_question.expected_preview: The gateway protocol schemas are organized in separate files under src/gateway/protocol/schema/: age
per_question.answer_preview: Gateway protocol schema modules define RPC message types. They are aggregated via index exports in src/gateway/protocol/schema/index.ts for use in server-methods.ts.

per_question.expected_preview: src/agents/tools/common.ts (lines 1-100) implements a parameter extraction pattern for agent tools.
per_question.answer_preview: src/agents/tools/common.ts exports: readStringParam, readNumberParam, readReactionParams for type-safe parameter extraction from tool inputs.

per_question.answer_preview: Channel plugin config adapters resolve account-specific settings by looking up config[accountId] or falling back to default account settings in resolveAllowFrom/formatAllowFrom patterns.

per_question.answer_preview: Gateway 'agent' RPC method invokes the agent command handler from src/commands/agent/. The handler is registered in server-methods.ts gateway request handlers.

per_question.expected_preview: The getChannelDock function at src/channels/dock.ts:434 first checks the static DOCKS map for core c
per_question.answer_preview: listChannelDocks() merges DOCKS (core) with plugin channels from requireActivePluginRegistry().channels, using buildDockFromPlugin() for missing docks, sorted by order.

per_question.answer_preview: Agent lifecycle events are emitted during execution. agent.wait RPC polls job status or subscribes to completion events from the agent run registry.

per_question.answer_preview: Agent command service tracks execution context with runId and emits events: 'start', 'progress', 'end' via event emitters registered in server's broadcast system.
